exits.label: ◂ back out to the Night Market

npcs.name: Goodman Vell
npcs.title: a connoisseur of souls
npcs.hail: *(a soul of exquisite, dreadful composure reclines in the best chair with the languid contentment of a thing that has eaten well for a very long time — and your dead-touched sense, reaching for it out of habit, recoils, because there is no wound here to read, no desperation, no Hunger curdled out of grief; there is only a soul that was whole and ordinary once, discovered it could consume others to stave off its own fading, and kept doing it long, long after it needed to, because it found that it *liked* the taste)*

npcs.name: the Half-Taken
npcs.title: a soul mid-meal
npcs.hail: *(a soul sits too still in the chair beside the connoisseur's, fraying at one edge where something has been delicately, unhurriedly eaten away — a favourite memory here, a loved one's face there — and it does not flee, because the connoisseur has taken just enough of its will to leave it docile and just little enough to leave it aware, which is, you understand with a cold lurch, the whole refined cruelty of the thing)*

npcs.name: the Emptied
npcs.title: what the connoisseur leaves
npcs.hail: *(a hollow shade drifts in the corner, eaten down past memory, past name, past nearly everything — but not, you find when your cold touches it, past *all*; there is a single stubborn ember left, one thing the connoisseur found too sour to swallow, and the husk guards it the way a robbed house guards its one unburnt letter)*

variants.text: *(It does not rise. It regards you with a slow, appreciative smile, the way a gourmand regards an unfamiliar dish.)* …*Oh.* *(a soft, delighted exhalation)* Now *you* are something I have *never* tasted. *(it steeples long fingers)* Cold. *Returned.* A soul that went *into* the dark and walked *out* of it — do you know how *rare* a vintage that is? *(it leans forward, hungry, unhurried)* I have eaten *kings*, traveler. I have eaten *saints* — they taste of *self-regard*, mostly, a disappointing note. I have eaten the *grief* off ten thousand mourners and the *first* love off a thousand brides. *(the smile sharpens)* But a *Returned* — a soul that *remembers* being *un-made* and *survived* it — *(it nearly shivers)* — that I would *savour.* *(it gestures, courteous, to the chair)* But where are my manners. *Sit.* I so rarely get to *converse* with the meal. You'll forgive me if I find you *fascinating.* I have not been *surprised* by a flavour in an *age*, and you, cold one, are an *entire* new *course.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(It settles back, perfectly at ease, a predator that has never once in its long career met prey it feared.)* Speak, by all means. *(a languid smile)* I do enjoy a meal that *talks.* It seasons the thing — anticipation, you understand, is half the palate. *Ask* me what you like, cold one. I am *expansive*, when I'm *fed*, and I have fed *very* well.

choices.text: "What are you, Vell? Not a Hunger-fragment — I've met those, and they're starving wounds. You're well-fed and pleased about it. So what makes a soul choose to eat others?"

[INSIGHT] "I've met monsters with reasons all down this causeway — the forgotten, the desperate, the ones who eat because they're starving. You're the first that just *likes* it. There's no wound under this, is there. No tragedy I can witness back into a person. You were ordinary once, you found you could feed, and you kept going because it's *delicious.* I keep waiting for the part of you I can save. There isn't one."

*(the carrier of the dead, facing the consumer of them)* "Here's the difference between us, Vell, since you find me fascinating: I carry the dead the living can't, so they're not borne alone. You eat them, so they're not borne at all. We are the same reach — into the soul, past the surface — pointed exactly opposite. And I have spent this whole causeway witnessing souls home. I'm not going to witness *you* anywhere. You don't want to leave. You want the next course. So this isn't a rescue. It's the first time I've met a thing I can only *stop.*"
"The soul beside you — the Half-Taken. You're eating it slow, on purpose, leaving it aware. Why the cruelty? Why not just finish it?"
"I've heard enough, Vell." *(turn from the parlor)*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(It smiles, pleased to be asked, and there is something genuinely, chillingly candid in the answer.)* …What *makes* a soul *choose* it. *(it turns a long hand)* You assume there must be a *reason*, traveler. A *tragedy.* That is the *sentimental* error of your *whole* kind — you believe *every* monster is a *wound.* *(a soft laugh)* Some of us are simply *appetites* that *educated* themselves. *(it leans in)* I was a *taster* in life. *Wine*, mostly — a *palate*, a *gift* for it, the *finest* in three cities. I died *ordinary*, came *here* ordinary, *faded* like the rest — and then, by *accident*, I *brushed* another soul's *memory*, and *tasted* it, and it was — *(it closes its eyes)* — *exquisite.* A *first* kiss, *forty* years *aged*, with *notes* of *regret* and a *long* warm *finish.* *(its eyes open)* And I understood, in that *instant*, that the *after* was not a *prison* or a *punishment* — it was a *cellar.* Stocked with the *rarest* vintages in *creation*: the *lived* *experience* of *every* soul that *ever* *was.* *(it spreads its hands, serene)* I am *not* a *Hunger*, cold one. The *Hunger* is a *starving* *animal.* I am a *connoisseur.* I eat *not* to *survive* but to *savour*, and I have *refined* my *palate* for *three* *centuries*, and I would *no* more *stop* than you would ask a *gourmand* to *stop* *tasting.* *(a courteous smile)* It is the *one* *pleasure* the *dead* are *left.* I simply had the *wit* to *pursue* it.

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(For the first time the languid composure pauses — not in fear, but in a connoisseur's appreciation of a sharp palate.)* …*'There* isn't *one.'* *(it inclines its head, almost respectful)* *Bravo*, cold one. *Truly.* You have done the *one* thing the *sentimental* never *manage*: you have *looked* for the *wound*, *failed* to *find* it, and *believed* the *failure* instead of *inventing* a *wound* to *comfort* *yourself.* *(it smiles)* *Most* souls *cannot.* They *need* me to be a *tragedy* — a *victim* who *became* a *monster* — because the *alternative* is *unbearable* to them: that a *perfectly* *ordinary* soul, *handed* an *appetite* and *no* *reason* *not* to *indulge* it, will *indulge* it, *happily*, *forever.* *(it spreads its hands)* You are *correct.* There is *no* *part* of me to *save.* I am not *broken.* I am *working* *exactly* as a *soul* *works* when you *remove* every *consequence* and *leave* only the *pleasure.* *(softly, dreadfully)* I am what *any* of them would *become*, given my *gift* and my *centuries* and the *honesty* to *enjoy* it. *(the smile returns)* So. You *cannot* *witness* me *home.* You *cannot* *redeem* what *does* not *wish* to *be* *redeemed.* *(it gestures, almost kindly)* The *question* you *should* be *asking*, cold one — the *only* one *left* — is *not* *'how* do I *save* him?' It is *'what* do I *do* about a *thing* I *cannot?'* *That* one, I confess, I am *curious* to watch you *answer.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(It goes very still — and then, slowly, it *applauds*, three soft taps of long fingers, genuinely delighted.)* …*'The* *same* *reach*, *pointed* *opposite.'* *(it savours it)* *Oh*, that is *good.* That is *better* than *good* — that is *TRUE*, and *truth* is the *rarest* seasoning of *all.* *(it leans forward, hungry in a new way)* You *understand* me, cold one. You *carry*; I *consume.* You are the *connoisseur's* *exact* *inverse* — a soul that *reaches* into the *dead* to *hold* them *whole* — and do you *know* what that *makes* you, to a *palate* like *mine?* *(its eyes gleam)* The *single* *most* *valuable* *meal* in *all* *creation.* Not for your *memories* — though they'd be *fine* — but for the *thing* you *do*, the *carrying* *itself.* If I *ate* a *soul* that *holds* the *dead*, I would *taste* not *one* life but *all* the *lives* it *carries.* *(it rises, for the first time, unhurried)* You came to *stop* me, you say. *No* *witness*, *no* *rescue* — just a *stopping.* *(a courteous, terrible smile)* But you have *miscounted* the *room*, cold one. You did not *walk* into a *parlor* to *stop* a *predator.* You *walked* into a *cellar* and *announced* yourself the *finest* *bottle* on the *shelf.* *(it spreads its hands)* *Now.* Shall we see which of us is the *meal?* I do *so* enjoy a *course* that *fights* *back.* It *aerates* the *flavour.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(It smiles, indulgent, and lets your accusation slide off like wine off glass.)* …*'No* *wound.'* *(a soft laugh)* You *say* it as though you've *seen* *clean* *through* me — but you've only *seen* what I *chose* to *plate* for you, cold one. *(it turns a hand)* Perhaps there *is* a *wound. Perhaps* I am a *tragedy* after *all* — a *gentle* *taster* *driven* to *monstrousness* by some *grief* you'd *weep* to *hear.* *(its eyes gleam)* Or *perhaps* I am *exactly* the *appetite* I *appear*, and the *'tragedy'* is a *garnish* I *offer* the *sentimental* so they *leave* me *fed* and *consoled* both. *(a courteous smile)* You *cannot* *tell*, can you. *That* is the *connoisseur's* *finest* *trick*, cold one — not the *eating*, but the *doubt.* *Leave* a *rescuer* *unsure* whether there's a *soul* to *save*, and the *rescuer* *hesitates*, and *hesitation* — *(it nearly purrs)* — *hesitation* is a *flavour* too. *Look* *closer*, if you *dare.* *Decide* what I *am.* I'll *wait.* The *deciding* is *delicious* *either* *way.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(It glances fondly at the half-eaten soul beside it, the way one might regard a wine left to breathe.)* …The *slowness.* *(a connoisseur's smile)* You call it *cruelty.* I call it *technique.* *(it turns a hand)* A *crude* *eater* — a *Hunger*, an *animal* — *gulps.* Takes the *whole* soul at *once*, *bolts* it, *tastes* *nothing.* *Wasteful.* *(it shakes its head)* But a *connoisseur* *savours.* I take the *first* *love* *first* — the *brightest* note — and let the soul *feel* its *absence*, because a *memory* *tastes* *sweetest* when its *owner* *knows* it's *gone* and *grieves* it. *(softly, dreadfully)* The *awareness* is the *seasoning*, cold one. A soul eaten *aware* is a soul that *flavours* its *own* *consumption* with *despair*, and *despair* — *aged* *despair*, *drawn* *out* over *years* — is the *finest* *vintage* the *after* *produces.* *(it meets your eyes)* So *no*, I do not *finish* it. I *would* not *dream* of it. I will *eat* the *Half-Taken* over *decades*, *one* *precious* *note* at a *time*, and it will *feel* *every* *swallow*, and that is *not* a *flaw* in my *method.* It is the *whole* *art* of it. *(a serene smile)* You find this *monstrous.* I find it *refined.* The *difference* between us, cold one, is *merely* a *matter* of *taste.* And I have *spent* *three* *centuries* *cultivating* *mine.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vell
nodes.text: *(It inclines its head, unbothered, settling back into its chair like a cat into a sunbeam.)* …*Leaving.* *(a soft, knowing smile)* Of *course.* *Most* do, once they *understand* there is *nothing* *here* to *save.* It *offends* the *rescuer's* *appetite* — *(a dry laugh)* — we *all* have our *hungers*, cold one; *yours* is to *fix* *things*, and a thing that *cannot* be *fixed* leaves *you* as *empty* as a *missed* *meal* leaves *me.* *(it waves a languid hand)* *Go*, then. But *know* this, as you *go*: the *Half-Taken* will *still* be *here* when you've *left*, and I will *still* be *savouring* it, *one* *aware* *swallow* at a *time*, and *your* *leaving* will *change* *nothing* *except* to *spare* you the *discomfort* of *watching.* *(softly, the blade under the courtesy)* That is the *thing* about *evil* that *isn't* a *wound*, cold one. You *cannot* *witness* it *home*, and you *cannot* *bear* to *watch* it, and so you *leave* — and it *dines* *on*, *uninterrupted*, *grateful* for your *delicacy.* *Run* *along.* I'll *keep.* I *always* *keep.* A *well-fed* *thing* is *very* *patient.*

variants.text: *(It turns to you slowly — the connoisseur has left it just enough will to turn — and its voice is the flat, dreamy calm of a soul being eaten gently and aware of every bite.)* …don't. *(barely)* Don't — *trouble* yourself. *(it almost smiles, drugged-gentle)* I'm — it's *alright.* He's *very* *gentle* about it. *Vell.* He takes — he takes the *good* parts *first*, and it *hurts*, but it's a — a *soft* hurt, like — like *pressing* a *bruise.* *(it frowns, reaching for something gone)* I had a — there was a *daughter.* I *think.* He took her *face* last *week.* I *know* I *had* one because there's a — a *shape*, a *missing* shape, where she — *(it trails off)* …you should *go*, cold one. He'll *taste* you *wanting* to *help*, and *wanting-to-help* is a *flavour* he *likes*, and you'll just — you'll just be *next.* *(barely)* That's how he — that's how he *gets* you. He *lets* you *watch* him *eat* *me*, and the *watching* *seasons* *you.* *Go.* Before you're *seasoned.*

nodes.speaker: the Half-Taken
nodes.text: *(It sits in its drugged stillness, fraying, aware, and looks at you with the flat calm of a soul that has stopped expecting rescue.)* …*ask*, if you must. *(barely)* I've — there's *less* of me to *answer* with each *day*, but — *ask.* It's *nice*, a *little*, to *talk.* He *doesn't* *talk* *to* me. He *talks* *over* me. To *guests.* About my — about how I *taste.*

choices.text: "What's left, friend? He's taken pieces — but what's he NOT taken yet? Something he found too sour to swallow. Hold onto that one."
"Can you move? If I drew his attention — made myself the more interesting meal — could you get to the door while he's distracted?"

*(witness it before he can eat the rest — make it too vivid to swallow)* "Look at me. I'm going to do the one thing that ruins his meal: I'm going to *witness* you. Whole. Right now, before he takes another bite. The daughter whose face he ate — I can't give her back, but I can hold what's left of you so hard, so true, that you become too *vivid* to swallow. A connoisseur won't eat a soul that another soul is holding. It spoils the flavour. Let me make you inedible."
"Hold on, friend. I'm not leaving you to him."

nodes.speaker: the Half-Taken
nodes.text: *(It reaches, slow and dreamy, for the thing he hasn't taken.)* …What's *left.* *(a long pause)* There's — *(it almost smiles)* — there's *one* thing. He *tried* it, *early.* *Spat* it *out.* Said it was — he said it *'spoiled* the *palate', too* — too *bitter*, too *plain.* *(its frayed form steadies a little around it)* It's — it's not a *grand* memory, cold one. Not a *first* *love* or a *triumph* — those he *ate* *first*, they're the *sweet* ones. *(quietly)* It's just — a *Tuesday.* An *ordinary* *Tuesday.* *Rain* on the *window*, and *bread* in the *oven*, and the — the *daughter* whose *face* I've *lost*, *small*, *asleep* on my *chest*, and *nothing* *happening*, *nothing* at *all*, just — *being* there, *warm*, *unremarkable*, *safe.* *(its voice catches)* He *couldn't* *eat* it. It had *no* *drama* in it, *no* *despair*, *no* *grand* *flavour* — just *ordinary* *contentment*, which to a *connoisseur* of *anguish* tastes like *nothing.* *(it looks at you, and there's the faintest spark)* *That's* what's *left*, cold one. The *plainest* *Tuesday* of my *life.* The *one* thing too *happy* and too *small* for him to *want.* *(barely)* …*funny.* I'd have *given* it *up* *gladly*, *once* — a *boring* *Tuesday*, who'd *keep* it? And it *turns* out it's the *one* *thing* the *monster* *couldn't* *take.* The *ordinary* *good* *day.* *That's* the *part* of me he *can't* *reach.*

nodes.speaker: the Half-Taken
nodes.text: *(A flicker of something — fear, hope, the ghost of will — crosses its drugged calm.)* …*Move.* *(it tests the word)* I — *maybe.* He keeps me *docile*, not *bound* — he *likes* that I *could* *run* and *don't*, it's *part* of the — the *refinement.* *(it looks toward the door, then at you, frightened)* But cold one — if you *draw* his *eye* — if you make *yourself* the *better* *meal* — *(its voice shakes)* — he *WILL* *take* it. He *wants* you. I *heard* him. A *Returned*, a soul that *carries* the *dead* — he's been *talking* about *tasting* you since you *walked* in. *(it grips your cold sleeve with frail fingers)* You'd be *trading* *yourself* for *me*, and I'm — I'm *half* *gone* *already*, cold one, there's *more* of me *eaten* than *left*, and *you're* — you're *whole*, and you *carry* *others*, and if *he* *gets* *you* he gets *everyone* you — *(it shudders)* …*don't.* *Don't* *trade* a *whole* *Returned* for *half* a *nobody.* That's — that's *bad* *arithmetic*, and I *won't* be the *reason* the *causeway* *loses* the *one* soul *pulling* *names* off the *Wall.* *(barely)* *Find* — find *another* *way.* A *cleverer* one. *Please.* I'd *rather* be *eaten* than be the *bait* that *catches* *you.*

nodes.speaker: the Half-Taken
nodes.text: *(You reach into it with the cold — not to eat, the opposite of eating, to *hold* — and you take the frayed, half-gone soul and witness it whole: the lost daughter's missing shape, the ordinary Tuesday, the plain warm safe nothing the connoisseur couldn't swallow, all of it, held hard and true in the cold where you carry the dead.)* *(And the Half-Taken, feeling itself HELD — wholly, by a soul that wants nothing from it — begins, faintly, to come back.)* …*oh.* *(barely, wondering)* You're — you're *holding* me. Not *eating* — *holding.* *(its frayed edges steady, the fraying STOPS)* And he — *(it glances at the connoisseur, and something has changed)* — *look* at his *FACE*, cold one. He's — he's *recoiling.* The way he *spat* out the *Tuesday.* Because a soul that's *HELD*, *witnessed*, *whole* — it *spoils*, doesn't it. The *despair* he *seasons* his *meals* with — you can't *despair* when a *soul* has its *arms* *around* you. *(it grips your cold hands, the spark catching to a flame)* I'm — I'm *inedible* now. You've *RUINED* me. For *him.* *(a cracked, joyful sound)* He can't *taste* a soul that *knows* it's *held.* *(it looks at the door, the will returning)* And *now* I can — I can *MOVE*, cold one, the *docility's* *breaking*, because the *thing* that *kept* me *still* was *despair* and you've — you've *taken* the *despair* and *given* me back the *TUESDAY*, the *warm* *safe* *nothing*, and a soul that *remembers* being *safe* doesn't *sit* *still* to be *EATEN.* *(it rises, frail but free)* *Thank* you. You didn't *save* me by *fighting* him. You *saved* me by *making* me *too* *loved* to *swallow.*

nodes.speaker: the Half-Taken
nodes.text: *(It nods, slow, drugged, and a flicker of the old fear and the new, dangerous hope war in its frayed face.)* …*Not* *leaving* me. *(barely)* *People* *say* that. And then they *understand* what he *is*, and they *can't* *fix* it, and they — they *leave.* It's *alright.* I don't — I don't *blame* them. *(it looks at the connoisseur, then back)* But cold one — if you *mean* it — *(its voice drops, urgent through the haze)* — *don't* *fight* him. *Please.* *Everyone* who *fights* him *loses*, because *fighting's* a *flavour*, *struggle* *aerates* the *meal*, he *WANTS* you to *fight.* *(barely)* If you *mean* to *help* me — *find* the *thing* he *CAN'T* *eat.* The *part* of me too *plain* to *swallow.* *Hold* *THAT.* Not the *grand* *parts*, the *sweet* *parts* — he's *had* those. The *boring* *ones.* The *safe* *ones.* *(it grips your sleeve)* He's a *connoisseur* of *anguish*, cold one. The *one* thing he *can't* *consume* is a soul that *isn't* *suffering.* *Make* me *not* *suffer*, and you've *made* me *inedible.* *Come* *back* and *do* *that*, if you *come* *back* at *all.* It's the *one* *rescue* that *works* on a *thing* that *eats* *rescues.*

variants.text: *(The hollow shade turns toward your cold with the slow incomprehension of a thing eaten nearly to nothing — and then, finding the one ember it has left, it speaks, and the voice is small and clear and unexpectedly fierce.)* …he didn't *get* all of it. *(barely)* Everyone — everyone *thinks* he did. *Vell.* They *look* at me and they see — *nothing.* A *husk.* *Empty.* *(a small, fierce spark)* But he *left* one. *One* *thing.* Too *sour*, he *said.* *Spat* it *out.* *(it holds the ember close)* It's — it's a *grudge*, cold one. The *one* thing too *bitter* for him to *swallow.* I *hate* him. That's — that's *all* that's *left* of me. The *hate.* The *memory* of *being* *eaten* and *hating* the *thing* that *ate* me. *(its small ember flares)* He *couldn't* *take* it because it's *about* *HIM*, and a thing *won't* *eat* the *hate* *aimed* at *itself* — it's the *one* *flavour* that *bites* *back.* *(it fixes you with the ember)* You're *cold.* *Returned.* You *carry* the *dead.* Then *carry* *THIS*, cold one: the *husks* he *leaves* aren't *empty.* We've *all* got an *ember.* And *embers*, *gathered*, are a *FIRE.*

nodes.speaker: the Emptied
nodes.text: *(It guards its one ember, the single fierce thing left in a hollowed shade, and waits.)* …*ask.* *(small, clear)* I've *one* *thing* *left* and a *great* *deal* of *nothing.* *Ask* about *either.* The *nothing's* *quieter*, but the *one* *thing* — *(the ember flares)* — the *one* *thing* has *plans.*

choices.text: "You said the husks aren't empty — you've all got an ember, and embers gathered are a fire. Tell me what you mean. How does a fire of grudges stop a thing that eats?"
"How many are there? Husks like you, that he's left half-eaten with one bitter ember each. Where are they?"

*(the one who gathers the scattered, to the one who'd gather the emptied)* "I've spent this whole causeway learning that the forgotten aren't gone — there's always a who under the nothing, waiting to be looked at. You're proof: he ate you down past your name and you kept the one ember he couldn't stomach. So here's what I can do that he never reckoned on. I carry the dead. I can gather the husks — all of them, every ember — and hold them together until the grudges he scattered become one fire he has to face."
"Hold your ember, friend. I'll come back for it."

nodes.speaker: the Emptied
nodes.text: *(The ember flares, and the small clear voice sharpens with a purpose that three centuries of being eaten could not extinguish.)* …How a *fire* of *grudges* *stops* him. *(fiercely)* *Listen*, cold one. He *feeds* on *despair* — *aged* *anguish*, *drawn* *out*, the *finest* *vintage.* That's his *whole* *palate.* *(the ember crackles)* But a *grudge* isn't *despair.* A *grudge* is *despair* that *turned* *AROUND* and *picked* a *target.* It's *anguish* with *TEETH.* And he *can't* *eat* it — he *told* me so himself, *spitting* me *out* — because the *one* flavour a *connoisseur* of *suffering* *cannot* *swallow* is *suffering* *aimed* *back* at *HIM.* *(it leans close)* So here's the *plan*, the one I've *nursed* in the *dark* for *centuries* with the *one* ember he *left* me: he's *eaten* *hundreds*, cold one. *Hundreds* of *husks*, *scattered*, each with *one* *bitter* *ember*, each *alone*, each *too* *small* to *matter.* *(the ember blazes)* But a *Returned* — a soul that *GATHERS* the *dead* — could *bring* them *together.* *All* the *husks.* *All* the *embers.* And *hundreds* of *grudges*, *gathered* into *one* *place*, *aimed* at *one* *connoisseur* — *(fierce, triumphant)* — that's not a *flavour* he can *savour*, cold one. That's a *FIRE* he has to *STAND* *in.* A soul that *eats* *suffering* *drowned* in *three* *centuries* of *suffering* *aimed* at *HIM.* *(it fixes you with the ember)* You *can't* *witness* him *home.* You *can't* *fight* him *clean.* But you can *gather* the *fire* he *made* by *eating* us — and *let* him *choke* on the *one* *dish* he *spent* *centuries* *cooking*: *us, all at once, hating him whole.*

nodes.speaker: the Emptied
nodes.text: *(The hollow shade is quiet, the ember dimming with an old grief.)* …How *many.* *(small)* I *don't* *know* the *number*, cold one. He's *eaten* for *three* *centuries* and he *eats* *slow*, *savouring* — so *not* *thousands*, like the *Wall.* But *hundreds.* *Easily* *hundreds.* *(the ember flickers)* And they're — they're *scattered.* That's the *cruelty* of it, the *thing* that's *kept* us *helpless.* He doesn't *keep* his *husks* in *one* *place* — too *risky*, too *much* *grudge* in *one* *room.* He *eats* us and *lets* us *drift*, *hollow*, *alone*, *each* with our *one* *ember*, *each* *certain* we're the *only* *one*, *each* too *small* and *too* *scattered* to *do* anything but *hate* him *quietly* in the *dark.* *(it looks at you)* That's how a *connoisseur* *survives* what he *does*, cold one — *not* by *hiding* his *victims*, but by *scattering* them, so the *grudges* never *gather.* *(the ember flares)* It's the *same* *trick* the *WALL* *plays* — *scatter* the *forgotten* so they *can't* *add* *up.* And it *works*, the same way, for the *same* *reason* — *until*, *until* a soul comes *along* that *GATHERS.* *(it fixes you)* You're the *flaw* in his *whole* *method*, cold one. He *scattered* us *certain* *no* one could *bring* us *back* *together.* He never *reckoned* on a *thing* that *carries* the *dead.* *Find* them. *Count* them. *Gather* them. He *can't* *un-scatter* what *you* can *gather.*

nodes.speaker: the Emptied
nodes.text: *(The ember blazes up, brighter than a hollowed shade should be able to burn, and the small clear voice rings with three centuries of waited-for vindication.)* …You'll *GATHER* us. *(barely)* All of us. *Every* *husk*, *every* *ember.* *(it seizes your cold hand with frail fierce fingers)* Do you *KNOW* what you've — cold one, I have *NURSED* this *grudge* in the *DARK* for *THREE* *CENTURIES*, *certain* it would *die* with me, *unspent*, *one* *small* *bitter* *ember* in *one* *forgotten* *husk* — and you're telling me you can *bring* the *OTHERS*, the *hundreds*, and *hold* us *together* until our *scattered* *hate* becomes *one* *FIRE* — *(the ember roars)* — *YES.* *Gods*, *YES.* *Gather* us, cold one. *Carry* the *husks* the *way* you *carry* the *dead* — *all* of us, *all* our *embers*, *into* *one* *room*, *aimed* at *one* *connoisseur* — *(fierce, weeping embers)* — and let him *learn* the *one* *lesson* a *thing* that *eats* *suffering* can *never* *teach* *itself*: that *suffering* *REMEMBERS.* That *every* soul he *savoured* *kept* an *ember.* That the *scattered* can be *gathered*, and the *gathered* can *BURN.* *(it presses its ember toward you, an offering)* *Take* *mine* *first.* The *hate* he *spat* out as *too* *bitter.* *Carry* it, and *find* the *rest*, and *bring* us *all* *back* to this *parlor* *together* — *(the ember flares white)* — and we will *cook* the *connoisseur* in the *fire* he *made* of *us.* *Not* *witnessed* *home.* Not *redeemed.* *Just* — *stopped.* By the *hundreds* he *thought* too *scattered* to *count.* *That's* the *answer*, cold one. *That's* what you *DO* about a thing you *can't* *save.* You *gather* the *ones* it *couldn't* *finish*, and you *let* them *finish* *IT.*

nodes.speaker: the Emptied
nodes.text: *(The ember dims, but does not go out — it never goes out; that is the whole point of it.)* …*Come* *back.* *(small, certain)* You *will.* I can *tell.* *(the ember glows steady)* The *others* *who* *came* — they *left* and *didn't* *return*, because they *looked* at *Vell* and saw a thing they *couldn't* *fix*, and a thing you *can't* *fix* is a thing you'd *rather* *forget.* *(fiercely)* But *you* — you *looked* at *ME*, the *husk*, the *empty* *thing*, and you *SAW* the *ember.* *Nobody* *sees* the *ember.* *(it holds it close)* So you'll *come* *back.* Because a soul that *sees* the *one* *thing* *left* in a *hollowed* *shade* is a soul that *understands* the *one* *truth* the *connoisseur* *can't*: that he *didn't* *win.* *Not* *all* the *way.* *Never* *all* the *way.* *(the ember flares)* *Go.* *Mind* *yourself* — he *wants* you, he's *been* *wanting* you since you *walked* *in.* But *come* *back*, cold one. *We've* *waited* *three* *centuries* in the *dark* for a soul that *gathers.* *We* can *wait* a *little* *longer* for *you* to *be* *ready.* The *embers* *keep.* *Hate* is the *one* thing the *dark* *can't* *put* *out.*
